g.text: not yet supported (v1 = azure-container-apps only); stop
[ambiguity OR a GENERALIZED landmine — any choice whose correctness
depends on a fact NOT grounded in the inputs (access-path / purpose / app-config)]
SURFACE the gap, never bury it as a runbook STOP note
MANDATORY dialect confirmation — BEFORE any artifact (USE-DEPLOY-DIALECT)
resolve requested IaC dialect (Bicep | Terraform) — exactly one
MANDATORY deployment-purpose confirmation — BEFORE purpose-driven defaults (USE-DEPLOY-PURPOSE)
— NO silent default; never assume prod- or dev-grade
Purpose RECOMMENDS the sizing/posture; the operator
confirms. Secure-by-default is preserved — the vault
stays most-restrictive; any relaxation is explicit.
[each secured store (Key Vault, DB, ACR, remote state)]
name READER + WRITER; each needs an identity
and a network path the chosen posture ADMITS
[read-but-never-written, OR a writer with no admitted path]
BLOCKING: SURFACE the hole + connectivity options
The diagram is not "done" until every secured store has
BOTH a reader and a writer with an admitted path. A
complete-looking diagram with a missing write path is
exactly the failure this gate prevents.
write diagram.puml (UML deployment diagram; shows BOTH the
runtime read path AND the provisioning write path; doc-only stereotypes)
FULL PARITY regardless of dialect: Container Apps, ACR,
Azure DB for PostgreSQL (TLS; tier set by the confirmed
peers private) + BOTH role grants (reader Secrets User,
writer Secrets Officer), secret refs, ingress 8080,
pre-traffic migration job. Hardening that creates a new
requirement (e.g. a locked vault) surfaces the doorway,
write runbook.md (numbered steps; exact command, location,
env/secret; STOP before every state-changing or go-live step)
Enforces every atlas-ate-server invariant identically for
report written files; review the diagram
and run the runbook yourself. Executes NOTHING.
